reasons: Franco can isolate a target, giving Eudora a simple burst window after the catch.
Eudora can follow Franco's pickoff with immediate magic burst before the target escapes.

proof.summary: Franco starts the pickoff by catching or locking one target, while Eudora follows with single-target burst.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a damage dealer or fragile target.
Eudora waits near the pickoff angle instead of showing early.
failureCases: Franco catches a tank instead of a priority target.
Eudora is too far away to follow the hook or suppression.

reasons: Kaja can pull and lock a priority target, making Eudora's burst combo easier to execute.
Eudora can finish the target after Kaja creates a confirmed single-target setup.

proof.summary: Kaja provides the hard pickoff setup, while Eudora supplies the burst needed to finish the isolated target.
worksBestWhen: Eudora saves her stun and ultimate for Kaja's confirmed pull.
failureCases: Kaja pulls a durable frontline target.
The target survives because Eudora's burst is unavailable.

reasons: Tigreal can group and control enemies, allowing Eudora to burst a locked target inside the fight.
Eudora can add immediate damage when Tigreal starts a reliable crowd-control window.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates the crowd-control opening, while Eudora adds immediate burst to remove one target during the control chain.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal catches a priority target, not only tanks.
Eudora stays hidden until the engage starts.
failureCases: Eudora reveals too early and gets zoned.

reasons: Atlas can force enemies into a grouped control window, giving Eudora a safer chance to burst a key target.
Eudora can punish the target Atlas displaces into her damage range.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the initiation and displacement setup, while Eudora follows with targeted burst after enemies are pulled in.
worksBestWhen: Atlas catches a squishy target inside the group.
Eudora does not waste burst on a low-value target.
failureCases: Eudora's burst is interrupted or blocked.

reasons: Saber can lock a priority target with airborne control, giving Eudora a short but clear burst window.
Eudora adds magic burst after Saber starts the pickoff, helping remove fragile targets before they can escape.

proof.summary: Saber starts a single-target pickoff with airborne control, while Eudora follows with stun and burst damage during the target's vulnerable window.
worksBestWhen: Saber targets a fragile enemy damage dealer.
Eudora is already close enough to follow immediately after Saber commits.
Both heroes save their burst for the same priority target.
failureCases: Saber targets a tank or high-durability hero.
Eudora is too far away when Saber uses ultimate.
The enemy has defensive support ready before the burst lands.
